title: Library transfection
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"1ddff","text":"Be sure that the HEK293F cell line expresses BFP at a level of at least 98% before beginning a transfection (see Purity Sorting HEK293F Cells). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6rj6h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cr1jj","text":"Histopaque-1077 is a high-density liquid that allows for separation of live and dead cells in suspension by centrifugation. Live cells with intact membranes will not equilibrate with the Histopaque-1077 and will float at the interface between media and Histopaque-1077. They will look like white floating strands or clumps. Dead cells without intact membranes will equilibrate with the Histopaque-1077 and form a pellet at the bottom of the conical.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":124,"length":198}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Three days before the planned transfection, passage HEK293F culture and seed 2 flasks (more or less, depending on the number of cells needed). Do not add doxycycline or blasticidin to the Freestyle 293 media.
Count the line using a hemocytometer or cell counter and trypan blue.
Passage enough flasks at 6 x 10 6 cells/mL to transfect the next day. DO NOT add doxycycline or blasticidin to the Freestyle 293 media.
Warm Freestyle 293 media and Opti-MEM in a water bath at 37 °C for 00:15:00 - 00:30:00 .
For every 1 x 10 7 cells in each sample flask:
Take two 2.0 mL microcentrifuge tubes per sample and label with sample name and tube #1 and tube #2
Return the cells to the incubator with shaking ( 135 rpm, °C, ) at 8% CO 2 , 37 °C .
Plate cells for every sample to be transfected:
Count the cells using trypan blue and hemocytometer or cell counter.
1 x 10 7 cells in 9.3 mL Freestyle 293 media (WT, single variants, controls).
3 x 10 7 cells in 28 mL Freestyle 293 media (libraries).

Tube #1: Add 10 µg plasmid DNA to tube #1 For recombination, use a 1:15 mix of pCAG-Bxb1 plasmid to recombination plasmid (e.g. 0.67 µg Bxb1 + 9.33 µg recombination plasmid) Add Opti-MEM to tube #1 to a final volume of 335 µL and pipette up and down gently to mix
Tube #2: Add 315 µL Opti-MEM to tube #2 Add 20 µL 293Fectin to tube #2 Mix gently by pipetting up and down
Incubate the microcentrifuge tubes at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
1-2 minutes prior to incubation ending, remove sample flasks from incubator.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:30:00 .
Mix tube #1 into tube #2 by pipetting up and down gently.
Return cells to incubator.
Swirl gently
Add entire volume of tube #2 dropwise to each sample flask.

Check transfection control by flow cytometry to measure transfection efficiency: Take 1 mL of transfection control cells and 1 mL of negative control cells and put each into 5 mL flow cytometry tubes.
Add 1 µL AP1903 stock for every 1 mL of media to flask #1 and swirl gently.
Resuspend cells in 250 µL - 300 µL PBS + 1% BSA.
Aspirate
Spin 300 x g, °C, 5 minutes .
Resuspend cells in 1 mL PBS + 1% BSA (wash buffer) by pipetting.
Aspirate media
Spin 300 x g, °C, 5 minutes .
Add 1 µL of doxycycline stock for every 1 mL of media to each flask (for 10 mL media , add 10 µL doxycycline).
Passage each sample into 2 flasks, with 90% of cells in flask #1 and 10% of cells in flask #2.
Count all sample flasks using Countess slides and trypan blue.
Take samples to flow cytometry and measure fluorescence brightness and percent of cells in the transfection control sample that are brighter than negative control.
Return cells to incubator.
Take flask #2 for each sample (+dox, -AP1903) and prep for flow cytometry ( see Purity sorting protocol step 18 ) to estimate recombination rate Measure mCherry and BFP to estimate recombination rate
Kill off flask #2 cells at this point and continue forward with Histopaque Dead Cell Removal to remove dead cells from AP1903-treated cells (flask #1).
Histopaque Dead Cell Removal Remove Histopaque-1077 from the refrigerator and allow it to come to room temperature. Warm Freestyle 293 media in 37°C water bath.
Prepare a second set of 50 mL conicals for each sample.
Spin cells at 400 x g, °C, 30 minutes with no acceleration and no brake . This step will take about 40 minutes in total.
Slowly fill each conical to 50 mL total volume with fresh Freestyle 293 media.

Slowly layer in the entire volume of cells + media using a 25 mL serological pipette pressed against the side of the container just above the Histopaque-1077. Layer in cells extremely slowly and do not allow the layers to mix.
Add 15 mL Histopaque-1077 to each 50 mL conical.
For each flask that needs to have dead cells removed, prepare/label a 50 mL conical.

Swirl each flask gently before opening to take 10 µL of cells and mix with the trypan blue in the Eppendorf tube(s). Use 10 µL of this mixture and count cells for each sample using either a hemocytometer and microscope or a cell counter (e.g. Countess). Record numbers for all cells, viability, and live cells.
Remove flasks from the incubator and bring them into the hood.
Take a number of 1.7 mL Eppendorf tubes equal to the number of flasks that need to be counted and pipette 10 µL trypan blue into each.
Spin cells at 300 x g, °C, 5 minutes with brake and acceleration turned back on .
Dilute cells into Freestyle 293 media (10 mL total)

Using a P1000 pipette, carefully remove live cells from the interface until no more are visible, placing the live cells into the newly labeled set of 50mL conicals. This usually takes 5-8 tries to get all the cells.
When spinning is complete, carefully aspirate off the first 20 mL - 25 mL of media from each sample, taking care not to disrupt the cells at the interface.
Aspirate supernatant and resuspend cells in 10 mL media.
Place flasks in an incubator, 37 °C , 8% CO 2 , 135 rpm, °C, . Live cell percentage is typically above 75% after Histopaque selection.
Add 1 µL doxycycline per 1 mL media ( 2 mg/ml stock, final concentration 2 ug/mL) to each flask using a micropipette.
Dilute each sample with fresh media in 125 mL Erlenmeyer flasks to ~5-7 x 10 5 live cells/mL based on the most recent count.
Count cells again following protocol above to determine yield and purity.

Take flask #1 for each sample (+dox, +AP1903) and prep for flow cytometry ( see Purity Sorting protocol steps 17-24 on Day 2 ) to estimate recombination rate. Measure mCherry and BFP to estimate recombined percentage
creator: Sarah Heidl
